Submodules

news.admin module

class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin interface for the Article model.

Displays title, author, publisher, approval status, and publication date. Supports filtering by approval status and publication date, and full-text searching by title and content.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'published_date')
list_filter = ('approved', 'published_date')
property media
search_fields = ('title', 'content')
class news.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin interface for the CustomUser model.

Displays username, email, and role. Allows filtering by role and searching by username or email address.

list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role')
list_filter = ('role',)
property media
search_fields = ('username', 'email')
class news.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin interface for the Newsletter model.

Displays title, publisher, and publication date. Supports filtering by publication date and full-text searching by title and content.

list_display = ('title', 'publisher', 'published_date')
list_filter = ('published_date',)
property media
search_fields = ('title', 'content')
class news.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin interface for the Publisher model.

Displays the publisher name with a search field.

list_display = ('name',)
property media
search_fields = ('name',)

news.apps module

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'news'
ready()[source]

Override this method in subclasses to run code when Django starts.

news.forms module

class news.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating and editing news articles.

Fields:

title (CharField): The headline of the article. content (TextField): The body content of the article. publisher (ForeignKey): The publisher associated with the article.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']
model

alias of Article

base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.NewsletterForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating and editing newsletters.

Fields:

title (CharField): The newsletter title. content (TextField): The newsletter body content.

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'content']
model

alias of Newsletter

base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

name (CharField): The name of the publishing organisation.

news.models module

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing a news article.

title

Article title

Type:

str

content

Article content

Type:

str

author

Author of the article

Type:

ForeignKey

publisher

Publisher (optional for independent articles)

Type:

ForeignKey

approved

Whether the article is approved for publication

Type:

bool

published_date

Date the article was published

Type:

datetime

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

approved

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

independent_authors

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
published_date

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

publisher

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model extending Django’s AbstractUser with role-based functionality.

role

User’s role - ‘reader’, ‘editor’, or ‘journalist’

Type:

str

subscribed_publishers

Publishers the user is subscribed to (for readers)

Type:

ManyToMany

subscribed_journalists

Journalists the user is subscribed to (for readers)

Type:

ManyToMany

independent_articles

Articles published independently (for journalists)

Type:

ManyToMany

independent_newsletters

Newsletters published independently (for journalists)

Type:

ManyToMany

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]
articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

groups

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

role

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Override save method to assign user to appropriate group and clear role-specific fields.

Assigns user to group based on role and clears fields not applicable to the role.

Serializer for Article model to convert to/from JSON.

model

alias of Article

news.signals module

news.signals.article_approved(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]

Signal handler for when an article is approved. Sends email to subscribers and posts to X (Twitter).

news.tests module

class news.tests.ArticleAPITestCase(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: APITestCase

Test cases for the Article API endpoints.

setUp()[source]

Set up test data for API tests.

test_get_articles_for_subscribed_journalist()[source]

Test that readers get articles from subscribed journalists.

test_get_articles_for_subscribed_reader()[source]

Test that authenticated readers get articles from subscribed publishers.

test_only_approved_articles_returned()[source]

Test that only approved articles are returned.

test_unauthenticated_access_denied()[source]

Test that unauthenticated users cannot access the API.

class news.tests.ModelTestCase(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Test cases for models.

classmethod setUpTestData()[source]

Load initial data for the TestCase.

test_article_creation()[source]

Test creating an article.

test_custom_user_creation()[source]

Test creating a custom user.

test_newsletter_creation()[source]

Test creating a newsletter.

test_publisher_creation()[source]

Test creating a publisher.

news.urls module

URL patterns for the news application.

This module defines the URL routing for the news app, mapping URLs to view functions and class-based views for articles, newsletters, and publishers.

news.views module

class news.views.ArticleListAPI(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListAPIView

API view for retrieving articles based on user subscriptions.

Returns approved articles from subscribed publishers and journalists.

get_queryset()[source]

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
serializer_class

alias of ArticleSerializer

news.views.approve_article(request, article_id)[source]

Handle article approval by editors.

Approves the article.

news.views.article_list(request)[source]

Display list of articles based on user role.

Editors see unapproved articles, others see approved articles.

news.views.create_article(request)[source]

Handle article creation by journalists.

news.views.create_newsletter(request)[source]

Handle newsletter creation by journalists.

news.views.create_publisher(request)[source]

Handle publisher creation.
